pages.full_text:
so we headed for home. On the way we stopped at Wroxeter, (London, Cirencester, St Albans) 4th largest Roman town, 5,000 people & enjoyed the museum & reconstruction of a Roman town house.
June 9th
    8.45. Simon, Eve & Tinder are off to Annette's birthday party. Harry died yesterday. Annette (8th) John (May 31st) Max (12th) & Eve (6th) all have birthdays. Lizzie is visiting from Australia. Max, Tania, Kate, Dan, Annette, Lizzie, Pamela & John will be there. Max is about to move to Singapore. Tania's having another baby. The weather has been horribly cold & rainy for weeks, except when we were in Granada.
    The weather has held good. Warmish sun & cloud. Benjie 5/6 Franklin 3 (Max's) & Toby 3, Isadora 2 (Kate's) were here too, all good children. We had a super meal. Visited playground, evening concert at Wingfield church, old re-constructed organ, near Halesworth, Amici sang. Enthusiastic conductor. We slept on Annette's drive. Max in house, Kate
pages.notes: Top portion of page is in teal/turquoise ink and appears to be a continuation from the previous page, describing a stop at Wroxeter on the way home. The remainder of the page is in blue/black ink beginning with 'June 9th'. The year cannot be confirmed from this page alone; volume covers 2003-2019. Reference to 'Granada' suggests a recent trip to Spain. 'Wingfield church near Halesworth' is in Suffolk. Text cuts off at right edge — 'Kate' continues on the right page.

pages.full_text:
in hotel. Lizzie has been here 6 days.
Sunday we sat in garden sunshine then had hot dogs at Euston Agricultural Show. Tinder yapped, big & little steam engines & a steam bus. 2.45. We head for home. A good week end.

__About June 16th__
    Simon, Eve & Tinder went on a day trip to Pershore. Simon bought a battery for the van. We wandered around Pershore & bought a mirror for dressing up in the play room.
    The Abbey is beautiful. They allowed us to take Tinder in. Another man came in with his dog & it weed in the church.
    It was a wet day but we enjoyed it.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '9' visible in top-right corner. 'Euston Agricultural Show' — Euston is a village in Suffolk near Thetford; this is likely the Euston Estate show, not London Euston. 'About June 16th' is underlined as a heading. The Abbey referred to is Pershore Abbey. Year not determinable from page content alone; volume covers 2003-2019.
